Known for his bold, colorful style celebrating American pop culture, Morris unveils fresh pieces that capture everyday objects with energy and optimism. Art-loving families can meet the artist and explore his playful contemporary creations.

title: Rancho Days at the Dominguez Rancho Adobe Museum
description: Step back in time to explore what daily life looked like during Southern California's fascinating Rancho Era. The Dominguez Rancho Adobe Museum brings this historic period to life with hands-on activities and educational experiences perfect for the whole family.

title: Augusta Prelude

title: Amplify Your Authenticity - A Spring Equinox Healing Retreat with Jeralyn Glass, Maejor, and Niamh McCarthy.
description: Harness the transformative energy of the Spring Equinox at this healing retreat focused on resetting rhythms and planting intentions. Led by experienced facilitators Jeralyn Glass, Maejor, and Niamh McCarthy, participants explore authenticity and personal growth during this powerful seasonal threshold.

title: Tulum Thursdays at LouLou Santa Monica
description: Escape to Tulum-inspired relaxation every Thursday evening starting at 6:00 PM at LouLou Santa Monica. Bohemian elegance meets the restaurant's signature atmosphere for an evening of style and sophistication that transports you to coastal Mexico.

title: JAZZ A GO GO at LouLou Santa Monica
description: Live jazz performances fill the air every Wednesday evening starting at 7 PM at LouLou Santa Monica. Great music, amazing atmosphere, and interesting people come together for a memorable midweek evening out.

title: Saturday Rosé Brunch at Loulou Santa Monica
description: Named one of the 14 Best Buffet Brunches in the USA, LouLou Santa Monica's Saturday Rosé Brunch sets the perfect tone for weekends. Enjoy an impressive spread of food and drinks every Saturday starting at 11:30 AM in this stylish Santa Monica setting.

title: Safari Fun at Preschool Palooza: 2026-2027 Open House!
description: Shepherd of the Valley Lutheran Preschool transforms into a safari adventure for their annual Preschool Palooza open house. Young children can enjoy wild and fun activities while families explore the school and meet the learning community for the 2026-2027 school year.
locationName: Shepherd of the Valley Lutheran Preschool

title: LA Pen Pal Club - 3/21 Meetup - Monrovia
description: The LA Pen Pal Club gathers on March 21 to celebrate the joy of handwritten letters and share recent mail with fellow enthusiasts. This in-person meetup connects people who love the art of letter writing and the personal touch of snail mail.

title: Opening Reception for "Convergence: Contemporary Artists of Armenian Descent"
description: Celebrate contemporary artists of Armenian descent at the opening reception for "Convergence" on Saturday, March 21, from 5:00–7:30 PM. The Forest Lawn Museum showcases powerful and diverse artistic voices in this compelling exhibition.

title: Poetry Through Chaos: 2025-26 LBYPL Showcase
description: Young poets take the stage at the Long Beach Youth Poet Laureate program showcase on Saturday, March 21, from 2–4 pm. The Billie Jean King Main Library hosts this celebration of emerging voices and the power of spoken word.

Explore the art of visual communication and the stories behind these groundbreaking Japanese designs with expert commentary at the Japanese American National Museum.

title: Equip Yourself: Scammers Still Targeting Eaton Fire Survivors - FREE Prevention Workshop
description: Eaton Fire survivors and older adults can learn essential fraud prevention strategies at this free community workshop. The session covers practical steps to recognize and avoid scams specifically targeting those affected by the fires, helping families protect themselves and their loved ones during recovery.

This exhibition offers a fascinating glimpse into the future of professional stage and event technology.

The band's diverse repertoire spans genres and generations, creating a lively atmosphere perfect for families. This toe-tapping performance celebrates Irish heritage with music that everyone can enjoy.
locationName: First United Methodist Church of Pasadena

This cherished springtime tradition invites the whole family to witness one of Southern California's most iconic natural and cultural events.
price: Adults $20; Seniors: $17; Students & Children (5+) $14; Children 4 and under are free

Families can explore the fascinating science of mummification and discover ancient burial practices from cultures around the globe.

address: 1000 Vin Scully Ave, Los Angeles, CA 90012

title: Heritage Farmstead Easter Eggstravaganza
description: The Heritage Museum of Orange County hosts a festive Easter celebration complete with egg hunts, crafts, raffle prizes, and photo opportunities with the Easter Bunny. Special early access times are designed for children with special needs, ensuring every family can enjoy this springtime tradition.
address: 3101 W Harvard St, Santa Ana, CA 92704

title: 25th Annual Chumash Day Native American Powwow and Intertribal Gathering
description: Malibu Bluffs Park comes alive with a two-day cultural celebration honoring the Chumash people and Native American heritage. Families can experience tribal ceremonies, traditional dances, authentic arts and crafts, and historical storytelling that honors Malibu's original residents.

title: Suitcase Puppet Shows
description: The Skirball Cultural Center's newly reopened Noah's Ark exhibit features inventive puppet performances that bring flood stories from around the world to life. Creative storytellers use repurposed suitcases to create a unique theatrical experience that delights young audiences.

title: Mummies of the World at the California Science Center
description: Mummies of the World returns with over 30 never-before-seen specimens from South America, Europe, and ancient Egypt. This fascinating exhibition unravels the science of mummification through preserved human and animal remains, offering older kids and curious minds an engaging exploration of ancient preservation techniques.
address: 700 Exposition Park Dr, Los Angeles, CA 90037

title: Studio Ghibli's Ponyo Exhibition
description: Over 100 original materials from Studio Ghibli's beloved film Ponyo fill this enchanting exhibition, including art boards, posters, and hand drawings by Hayao Miyazaki himself. Families can explore the magical story of a goldfish and her friendship with a human child, plus enjoy special screenings and interactive workshops.

title: Saturday Mornings at Children's Book World

Hart Park comes alive with drop-in art activities, live youth performances, a timed egg scramble for ages 10 and under, a guided family hike, and a Spring Play Zone featuring egg-and-spoon races and hopscotch.
